style: warm transitional bedroom with wood furniture and neutral textiles
description: Infer a complete static indoor scene from the attached reference image. Use the image as the primary source for room type, style, object identities, object counts, sizes, poses, wall contacts, support relations, and left/right/front/back spatial relationships. Do not assume an office or any other preset scene unless it is visible in the image. TreeSAGE Flow 2 lays out rigid or approximately rigid objects only; pillows, cushions, blankets, bedding, duvets, quilts, bed throws, and other soft bed textiles should be treated as visual details integrated into the bed or sofa, not as separate scene objects. Includes beige curtains at the left window and a round flush-mount ceiling light.

_reference_spatial_order_groups.evidence: R08 is far left, R02 left of R01, R03 right of R01, R07 and R18 are far right; R09 rug is foreground under/in front of bed. Global depth order disabled by view-conditioned branch mode.
On R02, R04 lamp sits left/back and R15 alarm clock is to its right and lower/front. Tabletop depth order is handled by support packing and collision checks in this oblique view.
On R03, R05 lamp is left/back, R17 books are lower/front, and R16 plant is to the right of the books. Tabletop depth order is handled by support packing and collision checks in this oblique view.
On R08 dresser, R13 plant is left of R14 book stack near the window.
R12 botanical art is at the far left edge; R11 rod spans above and left of R10 window on the same visible left wall area.

R06 landscape art is centered over R01 bed, between R04 left lamp and R05 right lamp. Floor decor near a side-wall window is kept as a separate floor branch; image left/right against wall panels is not a hard wall-tangent placement cue.

pair_orders.evidence: For side-wall objects, higher reference frontness means closer to the viewer and lower room-y tangent placement.

For side-wall wall-mounted/window/curtain objects, the image edge side disambiguates along-wall front/back when depth samples are missing or too sparse: west wall uses smaller image-x as the front/outside low tangent; east wall uses larger image-x as the front/outside low tangent.

corner_affinities.evidence: Tall side-wall storage reaches high in the reference crop and has rear/deeper depth evidence, so it should occupy the rear side-wall corner.

fixes.note: Floor coverings are visual floor layers, not ordinary support parents; large furniture may partly overlap rug and floor.

_functional_spatial_clusters.evidence: Doors and floor storage on the same wall should be adjacent along the wall, not tangent-overlapping.
Window, curtain, and curtain rod should be handled as one wall fixture group.
Rug/carpet is a thin visual floor layer; objects may visually overlap it without support-tree parenting.
